name: LCA Cockpit — The Run-In
description: A steel box the size of a wardrobe, smelling of diesel and vomit, with a narrow armored viewing slit through which the beach appears as a strip of smoke and white water between two walls of grey sky. The engine is loud enough to feel in the sternum.
atmosphericDetails: The hull rings like a bell each time a near-miss mortar round detonates in the water alongside.
Through the viewing slit, the Belgian Gates are visible as dark crosses in the surf, some of them already tipped with the orange flash of a craft hitting a Teller mine.
The men behind the coxswain are silent except for the sound of equipment rattling and one man praying in a low, continuous murmur.
